stem: A education study measures average test score improvement across 5 schools, each containing multiple classrooms. school A has 20 observations with mean 52.4 points (within-school SD = 9.5). school B has 15 observations with mean 32.4 points (within-school SD = 7.1). school C has 20 observations with mean 68.6 points (within-school SD = 9.2). school D has 10 observations with mean 48.5 points (within-school SD = 7.7). school E has 15 observations with mean 63.3 points (within-school SD = 5.0). The between-school standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 7.6 and 18.9 points. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true average test score improvement for school A, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-school variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for school A: 52.5 points (shrunk from raw 52.4). Range [52.4, 52.5] as between-school SD varies from 7.6 to 18.9. Pooled mean: 54.3.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 54.3 points (weighted average of 5 school means).
Shrinkage factor for school A: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/20). At σ_between=12.6: B = 0.972.
Interval [52.4, 52.5] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (52.4) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (54.3) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
